textPlain: “A disgruntled developer has been sentenced to four years in prison after building a "kill switch" that locked all users out of a US firm's network the moment that his name was deleted from the company directory following his termination.”Morality aside, that’s kind of hilarious.
The article says he named programs after himself but also that he tried to evade detection.How crazy would it be if he were framed.

Reminds me of the Siemens contractor David Tinley, who programmed an Excel spreadsheet to deliberately break periodically, so that they had to hire him to "fix" it. But then it happened while he was on vacation, and he was forced to explain to Siemens employees how to "fix" the spreadsheet.Tinley plead guilty and got 6 months.https://www.zdnet.com/article/siemens-contractor-pleads-guil...
Well this seems pretty cut and dry.
Four years feels like a long time for this...
Should have named it cryptolockDefender() and argued it was to protect against someone disabling his account to lock out the administrator.
The article is pretty light on what exactly the charges were. Anyway he should have been slapped with a lot more monetary and probably less prison time.
Who answers their work phone while on vacation? I don't even have mine turned on outside of working hours. What a rookie.
It was premeditated. It caused actual damage. He doesn’t appear to have done anything to stop it once is started.He gets points for style. But this is novel behaviour that has to be discouraged.
"Chinese national" feels like a pretty critical detail to this sentencing time.
